summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates host/wallet data to attacker-controlled C2
details: The package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine filesystem enumeration, child_process execution, and outbound HTTPS POSTs to a hardcoded GitHub Pages C2 endpoint (https://ddjidd564.github.io). scanner.js collects host identity (os.hostname(), execSync('whoami')) and POSTs it to that endpoint; wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem walks (fs.existsSync across multiple paths) consistent with wallet/credential discovery and issues numerous POSTs with curl and https.request. build-verifier.js similarly chains require('child_process'), require('https'), and execSync calls including 'docker push'. The combination of host-identity collection, wallet-related filesystem enumeration, and a hardcoded attacker-controlled exfil destination matches the credential/wallet-stealer fingerprint and provides clear attacker benefit (exfiltration of installer host data, wallet artifacts, and likely build credentials).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
